meaning: n.大包,大捆,(指标准量货物,如稻草,麦杆)货物vt.打包,捆包,包装,灾难,痛苦,悲哀
examples: A farmer produced a bale of hay. One horse or another can eat it, but not both.
The country has pretty much defeated the boll weevil, and the Department of Agriculture individually tests every bale on obscure measures.
Have you heard of a "pee bale"?
David Cotterill grabbed the first before Bale set up Hal Robson-Kanu for the second.
Most dealers buy rugs by the bale, but Sudeith has a more intimate operation.
